# PROGRESS VAULT \- Project By [David Beltran](mailto:davidbeltran0804@gmail.com)

## Iteration 1 

1. ## Requirements 

### Functionals

**FR-01 (Creation):** The user can create a new goal with a name, description (optional), and an automatic creation date.  
**FR-02 (Dashboard):** The user can see the list of all goals and their overall progress in a principal dashboard.  
**FR-03 (Inspection):** The user can open a specific goal to see its full details and current status.  
**FR-04 (Edition):** The user can edit the name and description of an existing goal.  
**FR-05 (Deletion):** The user can delete a goal, requiring a confirmation step before final deletion.  
**FR-06 (Goal Types):** The user can choose between three goal types upon creation: Simple (text-only), Numeric (counters), or Checklist (sub-tasks).

* **FR-06.1 (Simple Goal):** A text-only objective with a standard complete/incomplete status.  
  * **FR-06.2 (Numeric Goal):** The user defines a target integer value. The UI must provide controls to increment or decrement the current progress toward that target.  
  * **FR-06.3 (Checklist Goal):** The user can add sub-tasks to the goal. By default, the system distributes the overall progress percentage equally among all sub-tasks.  
    * **FR-06.3.1 (Custom Task Weighting):** For checklist goals, the user can override the equal distribution and manually allocate a specific percentage weight to each individual sub-task.

---

### Non-Functional 

**NFR-01 (Performance):** The application must cold-start in less than 5 seconds on modest/low-end hardware.  
**NFR-02 (Data Legibility):** The data.json file must be stored in a human-readable, pretty-printed format, allowing manual editing by the user.  
**NFR-03 (Extensibility):** The user interface must be highly customizable through a theme/template system based on open-source, easily modifiable code.  
**NFR-04 (Architecture):** The codebase must strictly adhere to a layered architectural pattern separation (Commands / Models / Storage).  
**NFR-05 (Resource Consumption):** The application must maintain a lightweight memory footprint, consuming no more than 60MB of RAM during normal operations. (Esto asegura que Tauri cumpla su promesa en hardware viejo).  
**FR-06 (Persistence):** All changes will be saved automatically in a local data.json file handled by the Rust backend.  
**NFR-07 (Portability/Zero Dependencies):** The compiled application must run standalone without requiring any pre-installed runtimes (like Node.js, Java, or .NET) on the target operating system.  
**NFR-08 (Cross-Platform Consistency):** The custom visual styles and CSS transitions must render consistently at 60 FPS across Windows, Linux, and macOS without UI degradation.

---

2. ## Initial Project Structure

progress-vault/  
├── src-tauri/                      ← Rust Backend  
│   ├── src/  
│   │   ├── main.rs                 ← Minimal executable entry point  
│   │   ├── lib.rs                  ← Tauri app builder & command registration  
│   │   ├── commands/  
│   │   │   └── goals.rs            ← FR-01 to FR-05 (Tauri commands logic)  
│   │   ├── models/  
│   │   │   └── goal.rs             ← Goal, SubTask Structs, GoalType Enum  
│   │   └── storage/                  
│   │       └── json\_store.rs       ← Read/write logic for data.json  
│   ├── Cargo.toml  
│   └── tauri.conf.json  
│  
├── src/                            ← Svelte Frontend  
│   ├── app.html  
│   ├── routes/  
│   │   ├── \+layout.js              ← config SvelteKit (SPA mode)  
│   │   ├── \+page.svelte            ← Dashboard (FR-02)  
│   ├── components/  
│   │   └── GoalCard.svelte         ← Single Goal Card  
│   │   └── GoalDetail.svelte       ← FR-03, FR-04, FR-05  
│   └── lib/  
│       └── api.js                  ← Encapsule invokes a Tauri  
│  
├── data.json                       ← local copy of dev  
├── .gitignore  
├── package.json  
└── README.md

6. ## Wireframe Dashboard

---

7. ## Implementation Order & Progress Log

### 7.1 Planned Order

The implementation followed a layered, bottom-up approach:

**Step 1 — Models**  
 File: models/goal.rs  
 Purpose: Define Goal, SubTask, GoalType — what is a goal

**Step 2 — Storage**  
 File: storage/json\_store.rs  
 Purpose: Read/write data.json

**Step 3 — Commands**  
 File: commands/goals.rs  
 Purpose: Tauri commands the UI calls — create\_goal (FR-01) first, then the rest

**Step 4 — Frontend**  
 File: Svelte components  
 Purpose: UI that consumes the commands

Validation step: before building the frontend, create\_goal and get\_goals were tested directly via Tauri DevTools (window.**TAURI**.core.invoke).

---

### 7.2 Completed Work

**Backend — Core CRUD**

* FR-01 Creation (with automatic created\_at date)  
* FR-02 Dashboard data (get\_goals)  
* FR-03 Inspection (get\_goal)  
* FR-04 Edition (update\_goal)  
* FR-05 Deletion (delete\_goal)  
* FR-06 Goal Types — Simple, Numeric, Checklist  
* NFR-02 Pretty-printed JSON storage  
* NFR-04 Layered architecture (commands / models / storage)

**Backend — Interactive Progress**

* FR-08 Increment numeric progress (increment\_goal\_progress, capped at target)  
* FR-09 Toggle sub-task completion (toggle\_subtask)  
* Both commands registered in lib.rs

**Frontend**

* lib/api.js — JS wrappers for all Tauri commands  
* \+page.svelte — dashboard fetching goals via getGoals(), navbar (Config / Goals / New)  
* GoalCard.svelte — simplified facade; shows goal name and progress; \+1 button and checkboxes wired to backend, click opens detail view  
* CreateGoalModal.svelte — tabbed form (Simple / Numeric / Checklist), creates goal via createGoal() (FR-01, FR-06)  
* GoalDetail.svelte — view/edit/delete modal:  
  * View mode: name, description, type, creation date, progress  
  * Edit mode: name and description (FR-04)  
  * Delete with inline confirmation (FR-05)

---

### 7.3 Iteration 1 Status: Closed

All planned FRs/NFRs for Iteration 1 (FR-01 to FR-06, FR-08, FR-09, NFR-02, NFR-04) are implemented and functional end-to-end.

---

### 7.4 Deferred to Iteration 2

The following items were identified during Iteration 1 but are out of scope for its original requirements, and are deferred:

* FR-06.3.1 / FR-10 — Custom sub-task weighting (UI and backend command)  
* Add/remove sub-tasks after goal creation  
* Welcome screen (per wireframe v1.0)  
* Config button functionality (theme/template system, NFR-03)
